# Rome city guide

## Overview

Rome is an open-air museum where every street corner reveals another 2,000-year-old ruin, another gelato shop, another espresso bar where locals argue passionately about nothing. The food is religiously simple, the history is overwhelming, and the pace is deliberately slow. When in Rome, slow down.

**Best time to visit**: Apr-Jun and Sep-Oct for warm weather without August's oppressive heat and mass tourism.
**Currency**: Euro (EUR). 1 EUR ≈ 1.08 USD.
**Language tip**: Learn "Buongiorno" (good morning), "Grazie" (thank you), and never order a cappuccino after 11 AM.

## Neighborhoods

| Neighborhood | Vibe | Best for | Transit |
|---|---|---|---|
| Centro Storico | Historic, bustling | Pantheon, Piazza Navona, people-watching | Bus, walk from Termini |
| Trastevere | Bohemian, romantic | Trattorias, cobblestone streets, nightlife | Tram 8, walk from Centro |
| Monti | Hip, artsy | Vintage shops, wine bars, local dining | Metro B, Cavour Station |
| Testaccio | Local, foodie | Market, Roman cuisine, nightclubs | Metro B, Piramide Station |
| Vatican/Prati | Grand, religious | St. Peter's, Vatican Museums, upscale dining | Metro A, Ottaviano Station |
| Trastevere/Gianicolo | Scenic, relaxed | Sunset views, botanical garden, trattorias | Bus 115, Tram 8 |
| San Lorenzo | Student, alternative | Cheap eats, street art, late-night bars | Bus from Termini |
| Aventine Hill | Quiet, elegant | Orange Garden views, Knights of Malta keyhole | Metro B, Circo Massimo |

## Food

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description | Must-order |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Roscioli | Bakery/deli | Centro Storico | $$-$$$ | Part bakery, part deli, part restaurant. The carbonara is legendary and the cheese counter is a museum. | Carbonara, cacio e pepe, burrata |
| Suppli | Street food | Trastevere | $ | The best suppli (fried rice balls) in Rome. Crack one open and watch the mozzarella stretch. | Suppli al telefono, pizza al taglio |
| Da Enzo al 29 | Trattoria | Trastevere | $$ | The queue starts before they open. Classic Roman pastas done perfectly in a no-frills setting. | Cacio e pepe, amatriciana, tiramisu |
| Pizzarium Bonci | Pizza | Prati | $ | Gabriele Bonci's legendary pizza al taglio (by the slice). Creative toppings, perfect crust. | Mortadella pizza, seasonal specials |
| Tonnarello | Trattoria | Trastevere | $$ | Big portions, lively atmosphere, solid Roman classics. The tonnarelli cacio e pepe is their signature. | Tonnarelli cacio e pepe, carbonara |
| Trapizzino | Street food | Testaccio/Multiple | $ | Triangular pizza pockets stuffed with Roman braised fillings. Oxtail, chicken cacciatore, eggplant parm. | Coda alla vaccinara (oxtail), pollo alla cacciatora |
| Giolitti | Gelato | Centro Storico | $ | Serving gelato since 1900. The pistachio and crema are benchmarks. Near the Pantheon. | Pistachio, crema, dark chocolate |
| Dar Filettaro a Santa Barbara | Fried fish | Centro Storico | $ | A tiny spot famous for one thing: battered, fried cod fillets. Cash only, no-frills perfection. | Filetti di baccala, puntarelle salad |
| La Pergola | Fine dining | Monte Mario | $$$ | Rome's only three-Michelin-star restaurant. Panoramic terrace overlooking St. Peter's dome. Reserve far ahead. | Tasting menu |
| Antico Forno Roscioli | Bakery | Centro Storico | $ | The bakery arm of the Roscioli empire. Pizza bianca (white pizza) warm from the oven, sold by weight. | Pizza bianca, pizza rossa |

## Landmarks and activities

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Time needed | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Colosseum | History | Centro | $$ | 2-3 hours | Book the underground and arena floor tour for the full experience. The scale is humbling even 2,000 years later. |
| Vatican Museums and Sistine Chapel | Art/Culture | Vatican | $$$ | 3-4 hours | Book skip-the-line tickets online. The Sistine Chapel ceiling is at the very end of a long but incredible walk. |
| Pantheon | History | Centro Storico | Free | 30 min-1 hour | The best-preserved Roman building. The oculus lets rain fall inside. Free to enter, arrive early. |
| Trevi Fountain (Fontana di Trevi) | Iconic | Centro | Free | 30 min | Throw a coin over your left shoulder. Go at sunrise for photos without 500 people in the frame. |
| Roman Forum and Palatine Hill | History | Centro | $$ | 2-3 hours | Combined ticket with the Colosseum. Walk where senators walked. Palatine Hill has the best views. |
| St. Peter's Basilica | Culture | Vatican | Free | 1-2 hours | The world's largest church. Climb the dome (551 steps) for the best view in Rome. Free entry, modest dress. |
| Trastevere Evening Walk | Walk | Trastevere | Free | 2 hours | Wander cobblestone streets at golden hour. Every corner is a painting. Stop for aperitivo at any piazza. |
| Borghese Gallery (Galleria Borghese) | Art | Villa Borghese | $$ | 2 hours | Bernini sculptures, Caravaggio paintings, strict 2-hour entry slots. Book weeks ahead. |
| Piazza Navona | Culture | Centro Storico | Free | 30 min | Baroque piazza with Bernini's Four Rivers Fountain. Overpriced cafes but free to admire. |
| Aventine Keyhole | Views | Aventine | Free | 15 min | Peek through the Knights of Malta keyhole for a perfectly framed view of St. Peter's dome through a garden. |

## Nightlife

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Trastevere Piazza Crawl | Bars | Trastevere | $-$$ | Bar-hop between Piazza Trilussa and the side streets. Aperol spritz in hand, cobblestones underfoot. |
| Salotto 42 | Cocktail bar | Centro | $$-$$$ | Stylish literary cocktail bar near the Pantheon. Design books, soft lighting, excellent Negronis. |
| Testaccio Clubs | Clubs | Testaccio | $-$$ | Rome's clubbing district. Goa Club for techno, Akab for indie, Alibi for LGBTQ+ nights. |
| Freni e Frizioni | Bar | Trastevere | $$ | An old mechanic's garage turned aperitivo bar. The buffet spread at happy hour is generous. |
| Il Barretto | Wine bar | Monti | $$ | Intimate neighborhood wine bar with natural wines, small plates, and a local crowd. |

## Transit tips

- **Metro**: Only 3 lines (A, B, C). Limited because every dig finds more ruins. Covers major sites though.
- **Buses and trams**: Extensive network but unpredictable schedules. Route 40 express connects Termini to Vatican.
- **Walking**: Rome is best on foot. Most major sights are within 30 minutes' walk of each other.
- **BIT ticket**: Single ride valid 100 minutes on bus/tram and one Metro ride. Buy at tabacchi shops.
- **Pro tip**: Book Colosseum and Vatican tickets online weeks ahead. Skip-the-line is not optional, it's survival.
- **Water fountains**: Free drinking water from nasoni (little nose) fountains everywhere. Bring a refillable bottle.
- **Aperitivo**: 6-9 PM, order a drink and get a free buffet at many bars. Trastevere and Monti have the best.

## Useful Italian phrases

| English | Italian | Romanization |
|---|---|---|
| Hello/Good morning | Buongiorno | bwon-JOOR-no |
| Thank you | Grazie | GRAH-tsee-eh |
| Please | Per favore | pair fa-VOH-reh |
| The bill please | Il conto, per favore | eel KON-toh pair fa-VOH-reh |
| Delicious! | Buonissimo! | bwon-EE-see-mo |
| Excuse me | Scusi | SKOO-zee |
| Where is...? | Dov'e...? | doh-VEH |
| Cheers! | Salute! / Cin cin! | sa-LOO-teh / chin chin |
